codeReview.summary: Re-reviewing the current branch state resolves the original `fix-providerinstance-base-type` blocker. `ProviderInstance` now requires canonical `provider` while inheriting the rest of `ProviderInstanceBase` via `Omit<Partial<ProviderInstanceBase>, 'provider'>` (`packages/shared/src/provider-instance.ts:37-46`), `extractConnectionFromProvider()` now exports `provider.provider` directly (`packages/shared/src/provider-instance.ts:81-89`), and the registry only keeps legacy `providerType` as an input fallback while normalizing stored instances back to `provider` (`packages/extension/state/provider-registry.ts:54-80`, `206-210`). Current HEAD tests also assert direct `provider` behavior (`tests/unit/suites/provider-instance/base-type.test.ts:43-167`, `tests/unit/suites/provider-instance/features.test.ts:17-81`, `tests/unit/suites/profile/extract-from-provider.test.ts:33-42`). The earlier scope violation in obsolete commit `74ad48b` should not keep failing the milestone because that commit is not an ancestor of HEAD, the provider-authoritative code was rewritten into clean commit `1d7c344`, the version-only cleanup is isolated in `7c6cb22`, and the current branch has `0.4.60` in `package.json` plus both manifests.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `refactor-worker` completion gate still allowed a worker to mark `followedProcedure: true` on a commit that later needed version-bump isolation. The later cleanup fixed the branch state, but the skill/process gap remains visible.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/Users/sero/projects/browser-ai/.factory/skills/refactor-worker/SKILL.md:118-130` requires a final diff review with 'NO version bumps in package.json or manifest files' and says `followedProcedure: true` should only be set when all checks were actually satisfied; `/Users/sero/.factory/missions/fdd24bf3-fc74-45b3-a646-4179615c1e31/handoffs/2026-03-11T08-27-18-409Z__fix-providerinstance-authoritative-base__0e1c9f11-7c83-45b3-9f94-1b50542c1d0c.json:52-54` still reports `followedProcedure: true` for obsolete commit `74ad48b`, and `/Users/sero/.factory/missions/fdd24bf3-fc74-45b3-a646-4179615c1e31/handoffs/2026-03-11T08-38-51-043Z__fix-version-bump-isolation__aea49e8d-8944-4824-8d2e-4f1215b2dbf1.json:10-31` documents the subsequent rewrite into clean commit `1d7c344` plus isolated version commit `7c6cb22`.

summary: Pass. In current branch state, the original provider-instance base-type blocker is fixed: `provider` is now the authoritative shared field, extraction and registry normalization use it correctly, and HEAD tests cover that behavior. The only problem in old commit `74ad48b` was an obsolete bundled version bump; because that commit is no longer on HEAD and its code was rewritten into clean commit `1d7c344` with version cleanup isolated in `7c6cb22`, no blocking issue remains relevant to the milestone.
